chunks.en: My builds usually take around ten minutes, and the full test suite runs even longer.
chunks.ja: 僕のビルドはたいてい10分くらいかかって、テスト一式はさらに長く走ります。

chunks.en: While I wait, I grab a coffee and skim through Slack to catch up on messages.
chunks.ja: 待っている間は、コーヒーを取りに行ってSlackにざっと目を通し、メッセージに追いつきます。

chunks.en: When a build fails right at the end, I feel pretty frustrated because I've lost that time.
chunks.ja: 最後の最後でビルドが失敗すると、その時間を無駄にしたようでかなりイライラします。

chunks.en: One thing that makes my wait shorter is running the slow tests in the background early.
chunks.ja: 待ち時間を短くする工夫の一つは、遅いテストを早めにバックグラウンドで走らせておくことです。

outline: Opening: how long my builds and tests take
Point 1: what I do while waiting (coffee, Slack)
Point 2: how I feel when a build fails at the end
Wrap-up: one trick that shortens my wait

modelTalk.text: My builds usually take around ten minutes, though the big test suite can drag on. So I've learned not to just sit there staring at the screen. Usually I'll grab a coffee, stretch a bit, and check Slack for anything urgent. Sometimes I skim through my emails or jot down my next task while I'm waiting. But honestly, if the build fails right at the end, my heart sinks. I've waited all that time for nothing, and now I've got to start over. It's like watching your bus pull away just as you reach the stop. One thing that really speeds things up? Running the smaller tests first, before the whole suite kicks off. That way, if something's broken, I catch it early instead of ten minutes in. It's a small trick, but it's saved me loads of frustration.

chunks.en: To be honest, my builds usually take around ten minutes, so I can't just sit there staring.
chunks.ja: 正直に言うと、私のビルドはたいてい10分くらいかかるので、ただ座って画面を見つめているわけにはいきません。

chunks.en: While I wait, I usually grab a coffee and quickly skim through my Slack messages.
chunks.ja: 待っている間、私はたいていコーヒーを取りに行って、Slackのメッセージにさっと目を通します。

chunks.en: When a build fails right at the end, I get really frustrated because I've wasted all that time.
chunks.ja: 最後の最後でビルドが失敗すると、その時間を全部無駄にしたようで、本当にイライラします。

chunks.en: One thing that makes my wait shorter is running the slower tests in parallel on CI.
chunks.ja: 待ち時間を短くするために工夫していることの一つは、遅いテストをCI上で並列に走らせることです。

outline: Opening: how long my builds take
Point 1: what I do while waiting
Point 2: how I feel when it fails
Wrap-up: one trick to shorten the wait

modelTalk.text: Honestly, my builds usually take somewhere between five and ten minutes. Not forever, but long enough to lose my focus. So while I'm waiting, I tend to wander off and grab a coffee. Then I'll quickly check Slack, skim a few messages, maybe reply to one or two. To be honest, that's a bit of a trap. I get sucked into a thread and lose track of time. And when the build fails right at the very end? Ugh, it's the worst. I've been sitting there for ten minutes, and then it just falls apart. It's frustrating, especially if it's some silly typo I could've caught. That said, one thing that really shortens my wait is running tests in the background. I kick them off early, so they're already halfway done. Plus, I keep a little to-do list handy for those gaps. It helps me feel like I'm not just twiddling my thumbs, you know?
